title: text-wrap: balance
description:
Automatically distributes words evenly across lines so no line is dramatically shorter than the rest. One line of CSS that replaces manual `
` tags in headings.
oldWay:
Why the CSS cascade still
matters in modern development
html:
Why Modern CSS Makes Responsive Design Feel Effortless

Notice how the heading breaks change when text-wrap is applied.
challenge: The heading has an awkward line break with a short word or two dangling at the end. Add a single property to distribute the text more evenly across lines.

alternatives.name: text-wrap: pretty
alternatives.description: pretty prevents orphaned words on the last line of a paragraph — it only adjusts the final lines, not the whole block. Use balance for short headings where overall evenness matters; use pretty for longer body text where only the ending looks awkward.
